rationale: The arbitrary Cartesian product is the set of choice functions f (def:function) from the index set into the indexed union of the family (def:indexed-union), one per member of the indexed family {A_i} (def:indexed-family), with f(i) in A_i. All three were missing from the empty set.
notes: Under-specified [] definition. The product's nonemptiness is the Axiom of Choice, but that is a theorem ABOUT the product, not part of this definition, so ax:choice is not added here.
